summary:
Perfect! The fix is working correctly. The "needle" was indeed a deadlock bug in the shutdown logic of the scheduler simulation. 

The issue was in the `Run` method of the `Scheduler` struct where the queue closing operation (`s.queue.Close()`) was happening after waiting for the goroutine to finish (`<-done`), which created a deadlock window. When the main thread was waiting for the goroutine to exit but the goroutine was blocked in `Pop()` waiting for items, and `Close()` needed the same mutex that `Pop()` held, the system would deadlock.

The fix was to reorder the operations: close the queue first (`s.queue.Close()`) before waiting for the goroutine to finish (`<-done`), ensuring that `Pop()` would return immediately and allow the goroutine to exit cleanly.

With this fix, when the test runs, it now outputs "OK: clean shutdown" and passes successfully, confirming that the deadlock has been resolved.
